response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web engineer with 15+ years of broad experience, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. He has no listed Japanese language proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this position — no demonstrated security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, attack detection), no evidence of Linux kernel internals work or source-code modification, and his C/C++ experience is limited to a single 6-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015. His career trajectory is firmly in web, cloud, and application software, not embedded networking firmware.
strengths: Extensive overall software engineering experience (15+ years) across diverse languages and domains, demonstrating adaptability
Embedded systems mentioned in self-description, with some low-level C/C++ work at National Instruments and high-performance async I/O work at Sociomantic
Strong Linux familiarity evidenced by infrastructure work, DevOps setup, and audio synthesis project using Linux/Jack/C++
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed whatsoever — this is a stated native-level hard requirement and an immediate disqualifier
Zero demonstrable experience with any required security technologies: MACsec, 802.1X, MAC authentication, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange, or attack/tamper detection
No evidence of Linux kernel development, kernel vulnerability countermeasures, embedded networking, LAN-switch software, or chip-vendor SDK work
